programmes.show_description: For nearly four months the Hughes family was filmed around-the-clock.
A film of five separate comedy to drama segments - directed by Visconti, Bolognini, Pasolini, Franco Rossi and de Sica. The cast includes Clint Eastwood, Annie Girardot and Alberto Sordi.

A woman denies owing her grandmother money for extravagant Christmas shopping; she claims she played nursemaid for her family instead.
A young girl is dragged to the ground after her leashed dog takes off and allegedly attacks a neighbour's pet; she maintains that she only lost control for a moment.
A woman accuses her ex-friend of posting nude photos of her daughter on Facebook; her ex-friend says she's just mad she didn't get invited to a party.
While visiting her grandkids, a woman says she was hit up for loans by her daughter who doesn't seem to care about payback.
A woman sues her ex-lover for damages caused to a hotel room as a result of his temper tantrum; he claims her jealousy is to blame.
A woman is relentless in her pursuit of payback for an accidental shrinking of her $50 pants; she denies keying a car over the incident.
A woman suing for the return of her computer points the finger at her home-schooled niece who, in turn, points the finger at her imprisoned cousin.
A woman admits that her small dog was off leash and not within her reach when it was attacked and almost killed by a neighbour's larger dog.
A man sues his former friend for refusing to return 'Caesar' the German Shepherd after he took a trip to Guam wearing his $300 gold chain.
Find out what's happening in London today with all the latest local news, opinions and events.
In the US, a missing woman and her money starts a huge police investigation. In the UK, the shocking murder of a woman in her own home leads to a nationwide manhunt.
In the US, a grandmother goes missing. Surveillance footage captures last movements of missing woman.
A wealthy lawyer shoots his wife from the backseat of a car - was it a tragic accident or cold-blooded murder?
When the skull of a woman is found in a Texas canyon, investigators discover that she was stabbed. Can they track down the killer?
One for the Road: Analysing the evidence, the medical detectives solve real crimes using forensic techniques, alongside reconstructions (which may be unsuitable for younger viewers).

Chuck Norris is Walker in this modern martial arts western. Trivette's brother Simon is arrested by the Rangers, and finds out Simon has been told to kill him.
Thriller with Milla Jovovich. A US embassy official based in London goes on the run after being framed for a terrorist bombing, and discovers a plot to detonate another bomb in New York.
Action thriller starring Kevin Costner. A dying CIA agent takes on one last assignment while trying to rebuild his relationship with his estranged daughter.
Adventure with Jonathan Rhys Meyers. The tale of James Brooke, the Rajah of Sarawak, who inspired the Rudyard Kipling story The Man Who Would Be King and Joseph Conrad's novel Lord Jim.
Violent crime thriller with Nicolas Cage. When his brother falls foul of a ruthless mobster, a straight-laced man must go against the odds to save him with the help of an old friend.

A dog loses an eye while in kennel care because the animal was allegedly fed out of the same bowl as another pet and a dogfight ensued.
A young vacationer seals his fate when he offers video evidence showing himself sailing over a man's roof on his snowmobile.
A woman and her girlfriend claim ex-roommates backed out of their lease after an air quality issue that sent them all to a hotel.
Divorcees lay out 10K to hire the best possible lawyer for their child then dispute who owes for that costly decision.
A woman with a protective order against her ex-fiancé says she was too scared to call the police when he hit her vehicle with his car.
Armed with a bat and a Pit Bull, a woman's stroll around the block ends with an attack by an unleashed dog and alleged ridicule from the pet's owner.
An assault during a custody exchange at McDonald's takes place after a babysitter serves the mother of her client's children with papers alleging abuse.
A dog-walking dispute escalates into a full-blown assault when a woman pepper sprays a puppy and its owners; she claims they beat her up in return.
A man gets into an altercation with his roommate and claims he was shoved into a television; his roommate accuses him of being drunk and belligerent.

In Colorado, a man claims his brother has murdered his girlfriend. In Michigan, a young couple is found dead in their garden shed.
In Florida, the body of a teen girl is discovered. The police turn to the neighbourhood cameras. In Colorado, the last man to see a missing woman professes his innocence.
A young mother calls 911 claiming she cut her own throat in front of her ex-husband and child. Doctors say there's no way she did it to herself.
When a man is murdered in a bomb blast, investigators sift through the remains to hone in on the bomber. Tiny fragments of plastic hold the key to the case.
Strong Impressions: Analysing the evidence, the medical detectives solve real crimes using forensic techniques, alongside reconstructions (which may be unsuitable for younger viewers).

Chuck Norris fights crime and helps the community as the eponymous Walker. When the death of a friend's son is ruled an accident, CD investigates further.
Western with Gregory Peck. An ageing bank robber goes in search of his former partner, who double-crossed him - but finds himself forced to take care of a little girl.
Thriller with Samuel L Jackson. A trained black-ops interrogator and an FBI agent resort to extreme measures to coerce a terrorist to disclose the location of three nuclear devices.
